channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ethics of Writing Assistance: Comparing AI Tools and Ghostwriting
item.description: The article examines the ongoing debate over the ethics of using ChatGPT and other AI tools in writing, comparing it with the long-standing practice of ghostwriting. Ghostwriting, where a professional...

item.title: Manitoba Métis Federation President Responds to AFN Criticism of Self-Government Bill
item.description: David Chartrand, president of the Manitoba Métis Federation (MMF), has responded strongly to Assembly of First Nations (AFN) National Chief Cindy Woodhouse Nepinak’s call for the withdrawal of Bill C-...

item.title: RSS general secretary Hosabale explains Hindu civilisational identity and denies Hindu supremacism in US talk
item.description: Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale, speaking at the Hudson Institute in Washington DC, rejected charges of Hindu supremacism and sought to clarify the organisation...

item.title: Manitoba Premier Announces Grocery Tax Reduction Will Include Local Convenience Stores
item.description: Manitoba’s Premier has confirmed that the province's grocery tax reduction, initially applied to major supermarkets, will now extend to corner stores across the province. This policy aims to provide b...

item.title: Family member describes impact of parents’ killings as court proceedings against brother resume
item.description: The article could not be accessed due to a server restriction, so a full and accurate summary cannot be generated. Based on the headline, the story appears to focus on a family tragedy involving the k...

item.title: Considering a TFSA for Your Shares? Here’s What to Know
item.description: As people near retirement, they often look for ways to consolidate their investments, and one option to consider is transferring shares into a Tax-Free Savings Account (TFSA). The main advantage of th...

item.title: Sanwo-Olu swears in seven new permanent secretaries and inaugurates Lagos Health Service Commission
item.description: On Friday, Lagos State Governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu, swear in seven new permanent secretaries and officially inaugurate the Lagos State Health Service Commission. The event happen for the Banquet Hall...

item.title: WHO Gives Green Light to Malaria Medicine Specifically for Infants
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) don announce say dem don give prequalification approval to a malaria medicine wey dey specially designed for newborns and infants for the very first time. The medic...

item.title: Wealthy Nigerians adopt family offices to manage assets amid tighter tax regulations
item.description: As Nigeria government dey strengthen tax monitoring and push for more transparency through digital systems, many wealthy individuals don begin shift from informal ways of managing wealth to more struc...

item.title: Five suspects charged in connection with alleged rape of teenage girl in Gravesend
item.description: Two adult men and three boys have been charged following allegations that a teenage girl was raped at a private property in Gravesend, Kent. According to Kent Police, the alleged offences took place o...

item.title: How to help children understand and cope with Anzac Day services
item.description: Anzac Day services across Australia attract large crowds each year, including many children attending with families or school groups. These ceremonies are often solemn and reflective, and can raise co...

item.title: Manitoba to extend tax exemption on prepared foods to convenience stores
item.description: The Manitoba government, led by Premier Wab Kinew, has announced plans to expand its upcoming tax exemption on prepared foods to include convenience stores, not just grocery retailers. The measure, in...

item.title: Multiple Customers Report Financial Disputes with Lower Sackville Car Dealership
item.description: Several customers of Race Auto Group, a used car dealership in Lower Sackville, Nova Scotia, have come forward alleging financial irregularities and fraud. Lisa Marr, one of the affected customers, cl...

item.title: Elsie Hewitt's Background: A Look at Her Family and Early Life
item.description: Elsie Hewitt, a British-born actress and model, is known for her strong ties to both the UK and the US. Born in London on March 5, 1996, Elsie moved to Los Angeles at the age of 10 with her family. He...

item.title: Ramaphosa criticises South Africa’s exclusion from G20 while highlighting global inequality concerns
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has remarked that South Africa now follows developments in the G20 “by rumour” after being excluded from key engagements linked to the upcoming summit in the United States. S...

item.title: Farrer Byelection: Voters Reject Major Parties in Favour of One Nation and Independents
item.description: In the upcoming Farrer byelection, the major political parties are facing a challenge from One Nation and independents. Traditionally a Coalition stronghold, Farrer has seen voter dissatisfaction with...

item.title: Supreme Court applauds near 92% voter turnout in Bengal and high turnout in Tamil Nadu as sign of democracy
item.description: The Supreme Court has welcomed the record near 92% voter turnout in West Bengal’s first‑phase assembly polling as proof of a strong democratic process. A bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant, alon...

item.title: City Nature Challenge 2026 starts in Visakhapatnam despite high temperatures
item.description: The City Nature Challenge 2026, a four-day global initiative running from April 24 to 27, has begun in Visakhapatnam, with participants documenting urban biodiversity across the city. Despite the ongo...

item.title: 2Baba Wows Crowd at Vanguard Personality of the Year Awards
item.description: At the Vanguard Personality of the Year Awards 2025, Nigeria’s legendary musician, 2Baba, gave a remarkable performance that left the audience at the prestigious Eko Hotels and Suites in Lagos spellbo...

item.title: Gold Coast council intensifies enforcement approach as homelessness rises amid housing shortage
item.description: The Gold Coast is facing a growing homelessness crisis as local authorities ramp up enforcement measures targeting people sleeping rough, particularly around the city’s high-profile tourist areas. A r...
